steps.title: Use the consistent trend
steps.content: The researchers examined settlements across regions with different political structures, economies, and spatial constraints. Even with those differences, the same trend appeared: larger settled areas were linked to higher population density in core urban areas.

steps.title: Reject overextended claims
steps.content: Choice B speculates about regions outside Western Europe. Choice C makes an unsupported comparison between within-region and across-region variation. Choice D says the settlements shared spatial constraints, but the passage says constraints were regionally distinct. The answer is A.
